summary: Iteration 2 closes all five iteration-1 findings well: fresh output is now shown before the next choice (R9/R11/R18), the multi-step restart's paused-and-in-progress behavior is defined (R15/R18), the failure path has its own section (R24/R25), Done-before-convergence is resolved (R19/R20), and the two compound requirements were split (R16/R17, R31/R32/R33). Coverage of the ask's menu (Explore, Clarifying Questions, Ask Human, Repeat from the Beginning, Done), the step-through loop, and the Manager-race safeguards is thorough and each requirement follows the 'When X, Y should happen' shape. Two issues remain: an unresolved contradiction about whether the automatic loop can ever 'resume' without choosing Done, and an unspecified in-flight display for single-step menu re-runs. Both would force a downstream design guess, so they should be closed before the phase converges.

findings.issue: R5 and R26 both say the automatic loop should not run again 'until the person resumes or finishes,' and the assumptions section states 'Anyone who can view the pipeline can pause, resume, and use the menu' — yet no requirement defines a resume capability. R1 specifies only a pause control; R7's menu offers Explore, Clarifying Questions, Ask Human, Repeat from the Beginning, and Done, with no Resume item; and the stated stepping model (R21/R22/R26, and the assumption 'the automatic loop stays paused until they choose Done') says Done is the sole exit. So it is undefined — and internally contradictory — whether the person can resume the automatic loop without finishing: if resume is a real affordance it is unspecified (no control, no effect defined, no distinction from Done); if it is not, the 'resumes' language in R5/R26 and the assumptions is stray and makes the 'resumes' branch of R5/R26 untestable. Resume is the direct counterpart to the ask's central 'pause' verb, so this ambiguity should be resolved rather than left to design.

The in-flight display for a single-step menu re-run (Explore, R8; Clarifying Questions, R10) is unspecified. R15 explicitly defines a 'restart in progress' state and suppresses the paused menu for the multi-step Repeat-from-the-Beginning restart, and R9/R11 (success) and R24/R25 (failure) define the end states, but no requirement says what the person sees while an Explore or Clarifying-Questions re-run is still running — whether the menu is shown, disabled, or replaced by an in-progress indicator. R28/R29 prevent an actual overlapping run but govern system behavior, not what is displayed, leaving an inconsistency with R15's explicit treatment of the multi-step case and a gap a downstream designer would have to guess at.
